item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just don't think the Foundation has done enough science on this. What would happen if multiple people entered the room simultaneously? Would the video be split-screen?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Make SCP-4141-2 watch the video SCP-4141-1 watch by making -1 record and livestream the video through a streaming site.

Excellent concept, but it's still could use some work to revamp on how the foundation actually know the wi-fi works.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The concept is beautifully simple, but the execution is very dry. The otherwise unexplored wi-fi connection is too obviously an authorial solution to the issue of “how would the Foundation know?”, except that it paints the Foundation as far too credulous. Why is this not described as “shortly after entering, online messages appear claiming to be a person trapped in the room” - how does the Foundation know that these messages are genuine?

The explanation that AWCY did it was a bit lacklustre. Ultimately, SCP-4009 explored this concept in a much more interesting way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Although I enjoyed this immensely, I’d like to say that it could be slightly better written and that you should maybe read through it again and revise a bit. Otherwise this is golden. +1

Edit: I’d love to see a testing log, maybe with multiple people entering at once or an incident log of a doctor wanting to escape the stressful life and live in the room. How do the people in there eat? Also is there a toilet?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, this is a properly weird situation. I'm not sure where it's going, but the premise is well laid out, and I'm curious to find out just who's causing this torment, and why. Now to read the collapsible!

Greetings, and welcome to the show!

Oh, no.

+1.

("AWCY? sacrifices people's lives to feed their pretension" skips always send a chill up my spine.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this a lot, and the twist of it being connected to “are we cool yet?” is great. Well done. +1

item.title: I only had a mild existential crisis while writing this.
item.content:encoded: I'd like to thank a random post I saw a few months ago for putting this idea into my mind. Thanks, random anonymous guy, it was a pretty dang cool idea.
